chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَ الْإِمْسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The words (to be spoken) during the morning and the evening

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/225)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/225)

parts.text: أَبِي أَيُّوبَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ الْخَزَّازِ

text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ عَلِيَّ بْنَ الْحُسَيْنِ ( صلوات الله عليه )  كَانَ إِذَا أَصْبَحَ قَالَ أَبْتَدِئُ يَوْمِي هَذَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نِسْيَانِي وَ عَجَلَتِي بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ فَإِذَا فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ الْعَبْدُ أَجْزَأَهُ مِمَّا نَسِيَ فِي يَوْمِهِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Abu Ayoub Ibrahim Bin usman Al Khazzaz, from Muhamamd Bin Muslim, who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It was so that whenever it was morning, Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> begin this day of mine<sup>asws</sup> before my<sup>asws</sup> forgetfulness and my<sup>asws</sup> haste, in the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and whatever Allah<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires’. So whenever the servant does that, it would suffice him from whatever he forgets during his day’.
en.sarwar: 5. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from abu Ayyub Ibrahim ibn ‘Uthman al- Khazzaz from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) in the morning would say, “I begin in the name of Allah my day, before forgetting or hastening to other things, with the name of Allah and whatever Allah wills.” If a servant (of Allah) does this, he will be rewarded for that which he may forget that day.’”
